channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.eurogamer.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.eurogamer.net

item.title: Cyberpunk 2077 gets a free PS5 Pro upgrade today that brings PSSR and better ray tracing options
item.content:encoded:
Cyberpunk 2077's messy launch is a distant memory, and while public opinion on it has mostly changed, CD Projekt Red isn't ready to move on from the game just yet. Case in point: today's free PS5 Pro update.

This comes as a surprise because, when asked in late 2024 whether it would take advantage of the extra PS5 Pro horsepower, CD Projekt Red said it had no plans to.

According to CD Projekt Red's overview of what to expect in the patch, PSSR and ray tracing are top of the agenda.

PlayStation Spectral Super Resolution (PSSR), the PS5 Pro's own DLSS-like upscaling technique, boosts the resolution output of the game to deliver the sharpest visuals of Cyberpunk 2077 running on console. The game was already handsome on PS5 and Xbox Series X, but with the recent improvements PSSR has received, this was a key enhancement the game needed.

Of course, more available power also translates into better performance profiles. This time around, CDPR is delivering three different options for PS5 Pro owners:

  • 'Ray Tracing Pro' mode "enables all available ray tracing enhancements" while targeting 30 FPS (or 40 if you have a variable refresh rate-compatible display)
  • 'Ray Tracing' features "select ray tracing enhancements" while targeting 60 FPS
  • 'Performance' targets 60 FPS without ray tracing and extends beyond that limit on VRR displays. By removing the pressure of ray tracing, it also delivers a crisp final 4K image

According to the official post, CDPR's favourite mode is the second one, which finds a sweet spot between visuals and fluid performance that greatly helps Cyberpunk 2077's first-person action. That said, all three modes on offer here are attractive and a notable update over the base PS5 experience.

Digital Foundry has provided a deeper dive into the update and underlines that CDPR has prioritised image quality over extra ray tracing improvements like path tracing, which simulates how lights interact with materials and further grounds the visual presentation. While impressive, this PS5 Pro version doesn't quite reach the maximum height of its PC counterpart.

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/cyberpunk-2077-ps5-pro-upgrade-arrives
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: We have a zero-tolerance policy around cheating" - Bungie targets Marathon cheaters as it unveils new plans to combat them
item.content:encoded:
Marathon's development team has issued a statement about cheating in the game, pledging to improve detection and add better player reporting tools to the game.

Posted on X via the official development team account, the statement starts by acknowledging rising concerns around cheating in Marathon. In the days prior to this post, reports of cheating were circulating online, especially in high ranked lobbies. While cheaters have been popping up since the game's launch, it appears to be a growing problem, though the full scale of the issue remains unclear.

"We have a zero-tolerance policy around cheating. We're actively banning confirmed cheaters and expanding our telemetry and detection methods to better catch the aforementioned cheaters," Bungie's post reads. "Some of this work is live already, and more improvements will roll out over the coming weeks. Anti-cheat is a continuous cycle of monitoring, improving, and responding. This is an area that we will continue to invest in to protect the integrity of your runs."

In addition, more reporting tools are apparently coming, including easier reporting options in the game and official website, improved reporting for in-game harassment, and exploring options for in-game mail which communicates the progress of such reports.

Longer term, Bungie is also apparently "investing in better voice chat moderation tools", as well as anti stream-sniping measures. Stream-sniping being the practice of intentionally hunting down influencers livestreaming the game to ruin their match. This last issue could include an option to hide account names in-game.

Much of this statement is a re-emphasis of prior stances by Bungie, which was keen to establish a no second-chances approach upfront. Cheaters will still be permanently banned upon discovery of such behavior.

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/marathon-cheaters-statement-anti-cheat-reporting
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
